name: Rest
instructions: Take a rest day to recover. Focus on light stretching, hydration, and mobility work if desired. Avoid intense physical activity to allow your muscles to repair and grow stronger.

name: Smith Machine Shoulder Press
instructions: Set up a bench in the Smith machine with a vertical backrest. Sit upright, feet flat on the floor, and grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, just above your shoulders. Unhook the bar by rotating your wrists. Inhale to brace your core, then exhale as you press the bar overhead until your arms are fully extended but not locked out. Keep your head facing forward and avoid arching your back. Inhale as you lower the bar back to the starting position, stopping just above your shoulders. Maintain control throughout the movement to avoid bouncing the bar.

name: Reverse Snow Angels
instructions: Lie face down on a mat with your arms at your sides, holding light dumbbells in each hand, palms facing up. Engage your core and squeeze your glutes to stabilize your body. Keep your legs on the ground and your neck in a neutral position. Exhale as you slowly lift your chest and arms off the ground, keeping your arms straight. Move your arms out to the sides to form a T-shape with your body, then continue upward to form a Y-shape overhead. As you move, rotate your shoulders so your palms face down at the top of the movement. Inhale as you reverse the motion, bringing your arms back to your sides, then lower your chest and arms to the starting position. Avoid shrugging your shoulders or overarching your lower back.

name: Close Grip EZ Bar Curl
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent for stability. Grasp an EZ bar with a close underhand grip (hands closer than shoulder-width), arms fully extended, and the bar resting against your thighs. Keep your torso upright, shoulders back, and elbows tucked into your sides. Exhale as you curl the bar toward your chest, focusing on contracting your biceps and keeping your elbows stationary. Pause briefly at the top, squeezing your biceps, then inhale as you slowly lower the bar back to the starting position. Avoid swinging your body or letting your elbows flare out.

name: EZ Bar Curl
instructions: Stand tall with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding an EZ bar with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart. Let the bar rest against your thighs, keeping your elbows tucked into your sides and your torso stationary. Exhale as you curl the bar up toward your chest, focusing on squeezing your biceps at the top of the movement. Keep your wrists straight and avoid using momentum by swinging your body. Inhale as you slowly lower the bar back to the starting position, maintaining tension in your biceps. Do not let the bar rest on your thighs at the bottom.

name: Cable Flies
instructions: Set the cable machine pulleys to chest height. Stand in the center, grabbing a handle in each hand with an overhand grip. Step forward into a staggered stance (one foot slightly ahead), keeping a slight bend in your elbows. Engage your core and maintain a slight forward lean. Exhale as you squeeze your chest muscles and bring the handles together in front of your chest, arms forming a wide arc. Pause briefly when the handles meet, then inhale as you slowly return to the starting position, keeping the movement controlled. Avoid letting your shoulders shrug or your back arch excessively.

name: Seated Tricep Extension
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 90-degree angle and sit upright, feet flat on the floor. Hold a dumbbell with both hands by gripping the handle, palms facing up, and lift it overhead so your arms are fully extended. Keep your core engaged and elbows close to your head, pointing forward. Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary. Lower until your forearms are parallel to the ground or just below. Exhale as you extend your arms back to the starting position, fully contracting your triceps. Avoid flaring your elbows out or arching your back.
muscleGroups: Long Head Triceps;Lateral Head Triceps;Medial Head Triceps

name: Back Dumbbell Pullover
instructions: Lie perpendicular on a flat bench with only your upper back and shoulders on the bench, feet flat on the floor, and hips lowered toward the ground. Hold a dumbbell with both hands above your chest, gripping the handle, palms facing up, with a slight bend in your elbows. Engage your core to keep your hips stable. Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbell back over your head in an arc, feeling a stretch in your lats and upper back. Lower until your arms are parallel to the floor, then exhale as you pull the dumbbell back to the starting position, squeezing your lats. Keep your hips steady and avoid overarching your lower back.

name: Chest Dumbbell Pullover
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet planted on the ground. Hold a dumbbell with both hands above your chest, gripping the handle, palms facing each other, and elbows slightly bent. Engage your core and keep your back flat against the bench. Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbell back over your head in a controlled arc, keeping your arms stable and feeling a stretch in your chest. Lower until the dumbbell is parallel to the floor, then exhale as you return to the starting position, squeezing your chest at the top. Avoid letting your lower back arch or your elbows flare out excessively.

name: Smith Machine Calve Raises
instructions: Place a weight plate or sturdy step in the center of the Smith machine, ensuring it’s stable. Step onto the plate with the balls of your feet, keeping your heels off the edge, and position the Smith bar across your upper back as you would for a back squat. Unrack the bar by rotating your wrists, keeping your legs straight but not locked. Inhale as you lower your heels toward the floor, feeling a stretch in your calves. Exhale as you press through the balls of your feet, raising your heels as high as possible and squeezing your calves at the top. Pause briefly, then lower back down with control. Ensure the bar stays balanced and avoid bouncing at the bottom.

name: Skullcrushers
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet planted on the ground, holding an EZ bar or barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart. Extend your arms straight up over your chest, keeping your wrists strong. Inhale as you slowly lower the bar toward your forehead by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms perpendicular to the floor and elbows tucked in. Stop when the bar is about an inch above your forehead. Exhale as you extend your arms back to the starting position, fully contracting your triceps. Avoid moving your upper arms or letting your elbows flare out.

name: Powerlifting Squat
instructions: Position a barbell in a squat rack at a height just below your shoulders. Step under the bar and place it across your upper back, either in a high-bar position (on your traps) or low-bar position (on your rear delts). Grip the bar firmly, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, and unrack it by standing up. Step back and set your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width, toes pointed out 15-30 degrees. Inhale deeply to brace your core, pull your shoulder blades back, and keep your chest up. Begin the squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees, keeping your knees tracking over your toes and your back neutral. Lower until your hips are at least parallel to your knees, then exhale as you drive through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top. Avoid letting your knees cave inward or your back round.

name: Cardio
instructions: Choose a cardio activity such as running, cycling, swimming, rowing, or using an elliptical machine. Perform at a moderate intensity where you can still talk but feel your heart rate elevated. Aim for a steady pace, keeping your posture upright and breathing steady. For example, if running, land softly on your midfoot and avoid overstriding. If cycling, maintain a smooth pedal stroke and avoid hunching your shoulders. Adjust intensity based on your fitness level.

name: Overhead Rope Extension
instructions: Attach a rope to the bottom pulley of a cable machine. Stand facing away from the machine, feet shoulder-width apart, and grab the rope with both hands, palms facing each other. Extend your arms overhead, keeping your elbows close to your head and pointing upward. Engage your core to avoid arching your back. Inhale as you lower the rope behind your head by bending your elbows, feeling a stretch in your triceps. Exhale as you extend your arms back to the starting position, fully contracting your triceps. Keep your upper arms stationary and avoid letting your elbows flare out.

name: Seated Shoulder Press
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 90-degree angle and sit with your back fully supported, feet flat on the floor. Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height, palms facing forward, elbows bent at 90 degrees and pointing downward. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Exhale as you press the dumbbells overhead, bringing them close together at the top without locking your elbows. Inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your torso. Avoid shrugging your shoulders or arching your back during the movement.

name: Close Grip Lat Pull Down
instructions: Sit at a lat pull-down machine and adjust the seat so your thighs are secured under the pads. Attach a V-handle or close-grip bar and grasp it with a neutral or underhand grip, hands closer than shoulder-width. Sit upright, feet flat on the floor, and engage your core. Lean back slightly (about 20 degrees) and pull your shoulder blades down and back. Exhale as you pull the handle down to the top of your chest, driving your elbows down and squeezing your lats. Pause briefly, then inhale as you return the handle to the starting position with control. Avoid arching your back or using momentum to pull the weight.

name: Tricep Bench Dips
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench with your hands gripping the edge, fingers pointing forward, and feet flat on the floor, knees bent at 90 degrees (or legs extended for more challenge). Slide your hips off the bench, keeping your arms straight and shoulders away from your ears. Inhale as you bend your elbows to lower your body, stopping when your upper arms are parallel to the ground or you feel a stretch in your shoulders. Exhale as you press through your hands to extend your arms and return to the starting position, focusing on your triceps. Keep your torso upright and avoid shrugging your shoulders.

name: Face Pull
instructions: Set a cable machine with a rope attachment at upper chest height. Stand facing the machine, feet shoulder-width apart, and grab the rope with both hands, palms facing each other. Step back until your arms are fully extended, then lean back slightly (about 20 degrees) and engage your core. Pull the rope toward your face, flaring your elbows out to the sides and aiming the handles toward your forehead. Squeeze your rear delts and upper back at the end of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position. Keep your shoulders down and avoid using your traps excessively.

name: Chin Ups
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with a supinated (underhand) grip, hands shoulder-width apart, arms fully extended. Engage your core and squeeze your glutes to stabilize your body. Pull your shoulder blades down and back to initiate the movement. Exhale as you pull your body up, driving your elbows down until your chin is above the bar and your lats are fully contracted. Inhale as you slowly lower yourself back to the starting position, maintaining control. Avoid swinging your body or kipping to use momentum.

name: Single Arm Dumbbell Row
instructions: Place your right knee and right hand on a flat bench, keeping your back flat and parallel to the ground. Hold a dumbbell in your left hand, letting it hang straight down, palm facing in. Engage your core and pull your shoulder blade back. Exhale as you row the dumbbell up toward your waist, driving your elbow back and keeping it close to your body. Squeeze your lats at the top, then inhale as you lower the dumbbell back to the starting position with control. Avoid rotating your torso or shrugging your shoulder.

name: Single Arm Overhead Tricep Dumbbell Extension
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the floor, holding a dumbbell in your right hand. Extend your right arm overhead, keeping your elbow close to your head and palm facing forward. Place your left hand on your hip for stability. Inhale as you bend your right elbow, lowering the dumbbell behind your head until your forearm is parallel to the ground. Exhale as you extend your arm back to the starting position, fully contracting your triceps. Keep your upper arm stationary and avoid locking your elbow at the top. Repeat on the other side.

name: Barbell Overhead Press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell at shoulder height with an overhand grip, hands just outside your shoulders. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Exhale as you press the bar overhead, extending your arms until they’re fully straight but not locked out. At the top, shrug your shoulders slightly to engage your traps, ensuring the bar is balanced over your midfoot. Inhale as you lower the bar back to your shoulders, keeping your elbows slightly forward. Avoid arching your lower back or leaning back excessively.

name: Donkey Calf Raise Machine
instructions: Position yourself on a donkey calf raise machine with your hips under the pad and the balls of your feet on the footplate, heels hanging off the edge. Hinge at your hips so your torso is parallel to the ground, keeping your legs straight but not locked. Inhale as you lower your heels toward the floor, feeling a stretch in your calves. Exhale as you press through the balls of your feet, raising your heels as high as possible and squeezing your calves at the top. Lower back down with control, avoiding any bouncing motion.

name: Cable Single Arm Tricep Pushdown
instructions: Set a cable machine with a single handle attachment at the highest setting. Stand facing the machine, feet shoulder-width apart, and grab the handle with your right hand, palm facing down. Pin your right elbow to your side, keeping your upper arm stationary. Exhale as you push the handle down until your arm is fully extended, focusing on contracting your triceps. Inhale as you slowly return the handle to chest height, maintaining control and keeping your elbow in place. Repeat on the other side. Avoid leaning forward or using your shoulder to push the weight.

name: Incline Bicep Curl
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 55-65 degree angle and sit with your back fully supported, feet flat on the floor. Hold a dumbbell in each hand, arms fully extended, palms facing up, and let your arms hang down behind your body. Keep your elbows close to your torso. Exhale as you curl the dumbbells toward your shoulders, focusing on squeezing your biceps and keeping your upper arms stationary. Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, maintaining tension in your biceps. Avoid swinging the weights or letting your elbows flare out.

name: Barbell Curl
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart. Let the bar rest against your thighs, keeping your elbows tucked into your sides and your torso upright. Exhale as you curl the bar up toward your chest, squeezing your biceps at the top of the movement. Keep your wrists straight and avoid swinging your body. Inhale as you slowly lower the bar back to the starting position, maintaining tension in your biceps. Do not let the bar rest on your thighs at the bottom.

name: Cable Chest Press
instructions: Set the cable machine pulleys to chest height. Stand in the center, holding a handle in each hand with an overhand grip, and step forward into a staggered stance (one foot slightly ahead). Position your arms at a 90-degree angle, elbows in line with your shoulders, and maintain a slight forward lean. Engage your core to stabilize your spine. Exhale as you press the handles forward, bringing them together in front of your chest, fully contracting your chest muscles. Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position, keeping the movement controlled. Avoid arching your back or letting your shoulders shrug.

name: Good Morning Dumbbells
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell with both hands at chest level, elbows bent. Keep a slight bend in your knees and engage your core. Inhale as you hinge at your hips, pushing your hips back and lowering your torso until it’s nearly parallel to the ground, feeling a stretch in your hamstrings. Keep your back flat and neck neutral. Exhale as you drive your hips forward to return to the starting position, squeezing your glutes at the top. Avoid rounding your back or letting your knees bend excessively.

name: Lying Tricep Extension
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet planted on the floor, holding a dumbbell in each hand (or a single dumbbell with both hands). Extend your arms straight up over your chest, palms facing each other. Engage your core and squeeze your glutes to stabilize your body. Inhale as you lower the dumbbells behind your head by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary and elbows pointing toward the ceiling. Lower until your forearms are parallel to the ground, then exhale as you extend your arms back to the starting position, fully contracting your triceps. Avoid flaring your elbows or arching your back.

name: Seated Leg Curl Machine
instructions: Sit on a leg curl machine and adjust the pad so it rests just above your heels, with your thighs secured under the seat pad. Grasp the handles for stability and keep your back against the backrest. Engage your core and tense your hamstrings slightly. Exhale as you curl the weight toward your glutes, squeezing your hamstrings at the top of the movement. Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position, keeping the movement controlled and your spine neutral. Avoid lifting your hips off the seat or arching your back.

name: Barbell Hip Thrust
instructions: Sit on the floor with your upper back against a bench, knees bent, and feet flat on the ground, hip-width apart. Roll a barbell over your legs so it rests in the crease of your hips (use a pad for comfort). Engage your core and keep your chin tucked. Exhale as you drive through your heels, lifting your hips until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees, squeezing your glutes at the top. Inhale as you lower your hips back down with control, stopping just above the ground. Avoid overarching your lower back or letting your knees cave inward.

name: Deadlift
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes under a barbell, and the bar close to your shins. Bend at your hips and knees to grasp the bar with an overhand or mixed grip, hands just outside your knees. Keep your back flat, chest up, and shoulders slightly in front of the bar. Engage your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back. Inhale to brace, then exhale as you lift the bar by driving through your heels and extending your hips and knees simultaneously. Stand tall, squeezing your glutes at the top, then inhale as you lower the bar back to the ground with control, keeping it close to your body. Avoid rounding your back or jerking the bar off the ground.

name: Towel Bicep Curls
instructions: Sit on the floor with your right knee bent and foot flat. Place a towel under your right foot, holding each end of the towel with your hands, palms facing up. Keep your arms by your sides and your torso upright. Exhale as you pull the towel toward your chest by bending your elbows, using your foot to provide resistance by pressing down on the towel. Squeeze your biceps at the top, then inhale as you slowly lower your arms back to the starting position. Keep your elbows close to your body and avoid using your shoulders to pull.

name: Arm Circles
instructions: Stand tall with your feet shoulder-width apart, arms extended out to the sides at shoulder height, palms facing down. Engage your core and keep your shoulders relaxed. Begin making small forward circles with your arms, keeping them straight and controlled, for the desired number of reps. Then, reverse the direction to make small backward circles. Focus on smooth, controlled movements to warm up your shoulders. Avoid shrugging your shoulders or bending your elbows excessively.

name: Machine Preacher Curl
instructions: Sit on a preacher curl machine and adjust the seat so your upper arms rest comfortably on the pad, with your chest against the support. Grasp the handles with an underhand grip, arms extended. Engage your core and keep your shoulders relaxed. Exhale as you curl the handles toward your shoulders, focusing on squeezing your biceps and keeping your elbows pressed into the pad. Pause at the top, then inhale as you slowly lower the handles back to the starting position, maintaining tension in your biceps. Avoid using your shoulders or back to lift the weight.

name: Band Reverse Pulls
instructions: Anchor a resistance band at eye level (e.g., to a door or pole). Stand facing the anchor point, holding the band with both hands, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, palms facing each other. Step back to create tension in the band, keeping your arms straight and core engaged. Exhale as you pull the band outward, bringing your hands toward your sides and squeezing your rear delts and upper back. Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position, maintaining control. Keep your shoulders down and avoid arching your back.

name: Bench Press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet planted on the ground, eyes under the barbell. Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width. Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and position it over your mid-chest. Inhale to brace your core, then lower the bar to your mid-chest, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your torso. Exhale as you press the bar back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms but not locking your elbows. Keep your shoulder blades retracted and avoid bouncing the bar off your chest.
genderAdjustment: Lighter Weight for Women;More Upper Body for Men

name: Bicep Dumbbell Curls
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand, arms by your sides, palms facing forward. Keep your elbows tucked into your torso and your shoulders relaxed. Exhale as you curl the dumbbells toward your shoulders, focusing on squeezing your biceps and keeping your upper arms stationary. Pause at the top, then inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, maintaining control. Avoid swinging the weights or using your shoulders to lift.

name: Bicep Push Ups
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands close together, directly under your chest, fingers pointing slightly outward to emphasize your biceps. Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels, core engaged. Inhale as you lower your chest toward the ground, keeping your elbows close to your torso and flaring them slightly backward. Exhale as you push back up to the starting position, focusing on using your biceps and chest. Avoid letting your hips sag or your shoulders shrug.

name: Bodyweight Squats
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Inhale as you push your hips back and bend your knees, lowering into a squat until your thighs are at least parallel to the ground. Keep your knees tracking over your toes and your back neutral. Exhale as you drive through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top. Avoid letting your knees cave inward or your back round.

name: Dynamic Stretching
instructions: Perform a series of dynamic stretches for 5 minutes to warm up your body. Examples include leg swings, arm circles, walking lunges with a twist, and high knees. Move through each stretch with control, focusing on increasing your range of motion and elevating your heart rate. For leg swings, stand on one leg and swing the other leg forward and backward, keeping your core engaged. Avoid bouncing or forcing the movements, and keep your breathing steady.

name: Cable Reverse Fly
instructions: Set the cable machine pulleys to chest height. Stand in the center, grabbing the left handle with your right hand and the right handle with your left hand, crossing the cables in front of you. Step back slightly, keeping a slight bend in your elbows and your core engaged. Exhale as you pull your arms out and back in a wide arc, squeezing your rear delts and upper back at the end of the movement. Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position, maintaining control. Avoid shrugging your shoulders or arching your back.

name: Calf Raises
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides (or use bodyweight for a lighter variation). Keep your core engaged and shoulders relaxed. Exhale as you rise onto the balls of your feet, squeezing your calves at the top of the movement. Pause briefly, then inhale as you lower your heels back to the ground with control. For added challenge, perform on a step with your heels hanging off the edge to increase the range of motion. Avoid bouncing or using momentum.

name: Chest Supported Dumbbell Row
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle. Lie face down on the bench with your chest supported, feet planted on the ground, and a dumbbell in each hand, arms hanging straight down, palms facing each other. Engage your core and pull your shoulder blades back. Exhale as you row the dumbbells up toward your waist, driving your elbows back and squeezing your upper back. Inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with control. Avoid shrugging your shoulders or using your biceps excessively.

name: Close-Grip Bench Press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet planted on the ground, eyes under the barbell. Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands closer than shoulder-width apart. Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and position it over your mid-chest. Inhale to brace your core, then lower the bar to your mid-chest, keeping your elbows close to your torso. Exhale as you press the bar back up to the starting position, focusing on your triceps and inner chest. Keep your shoulder blades retracted and avoid locking your elbows at the top.

name: Close Grip Dumbbell Press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet planted on the ground, holding a dumbbell in each hand at chest level, palms facing each other. Position the dumbbells close together, almost touching. Engage your core and keep your shoulder blades retracted. Exhale as you press the dumbbells straight up, keeping them close together and focusing on your triceps and inner chest. Inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to your chest with control, maintaining the close grip. Avoid letting your elbows flare out or your shoulders shrug.

name: Close Grip Push Ups
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands closer than shoulder-width apart, fingers pointing forward, directly under your chest. Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels, core engaged. Inhale as you lower your chest toward the ground, keeping your elbows close to your torso to emphasize your triceps. Exhale as you push back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms. Avoid letting your hips sag or your shoulders shrug.

name: Ab Routine
instructions: Perform a circuit of 5 ab exercises, such as crunches, leg raises, bicycle crunches, planks, and Russian twists, for 30 seconds each. Rest for 15 seconds between exercises. For example, during bicycle crunches, lie on your back, lift your legs, and alternate touching your elbow to the opposite knee while extending the other leg. Focus on slow, controlled movements and keep your core engaged throughout. Avoid pulling on your neck or using momentum.

name: Crunches
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent, feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart. Place your hands lightly behind your ears or crossed over your chest. Engage your core by pulling your belly button toward your spine. Exhale as you lift your shoulder blades off the ground, contracting your abs and keeping your lower back pressed into the floor. Inhale as you lower back down with control, stopping just before your shoulders touch the ground. Avoid pulling on your neck or using momentum to lift.

name: Lower Back Extension
instructions: Position yourself on a back extension machine with your thighs on the pad, feet secured, and hips just above the pad. Cross your arms over your chest or place your hands lightly behind your ears. Engage your core and keep your spine neutral. Exhale as you lift your torso until your shoulders, spine, and hips are in a straight line, squeezing your lower back and glutes. Inhale as you lower back down with control, stopping just before your torso is perpendicular to the ground. Avoid overarching your back or using momentum.

name: Static Stretching
instructions: Perform a series of static stretches for 5 minutes to cool down your body. Examples include a hamstring stretch (extend one leg forward, hinge at your hips, and reach toward your toes), a quad stretch (pull one foot to your glutes while standing), and a chest opener (clasp your hands behind your back and lift your arms). Hold each stretch for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply and avoiding bouncing. Focus on relaxing into the stretch to improve flexibility.

name: 12line Bench Press
instructions: Lie on a 12line bench with your feet secured, holding a barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width. Unrack the bar and position it over your lower chest. Inhale to brace your core, then lower the bar to your lower chest, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your torso. Exhale as you press the bar back up to the starting position, focusing on your lower chest and triceps. Keep your shoulder blades retracted and avoid locking your elbows at the top.

name: Diamond Push Ups
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands close together, forming a diamond shape with your thumbs and index fingers directly under your chest. Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels, core engaged. Inhale as you lower your chest toward the ground, keeping your elbows close to your torso to emphasize your triceps. Exhale as you push back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms. Avoid letting your hips sag or your shoulders shrug.

name: Dumbbell Chest Press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet planted on the ground, holding a dumbbell in each hand at chest level, palms facing forward. Position your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your torso, keeping your shoulder blades retracted. Exhale as you press the dumbbells straight up over your chest, bringing them close together at the top without locking your elbows. Inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to chest level with control, feeling a stretch in your chest. Avoid bouncing the weights off your chest or arching your back.

name: Dumbbell Deadlift
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand in front of your thighs, palms facing your body. Keep a slight bend in your knees and engage your core. Inhale as you hinge at your hips, pushing your hips back and lowering the dumbbells toward the ground, keeping them close to your legs. Lower until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings, keeping your back flat and chest up. Exhale as you drive through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top. Avoid rounding your back or letting the dumbbells drift forward.

name: Dumbbell Fly
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet planted on the ground, holding a dumbbell in each hand above your chest, palms facing each other, elbows slightly bent. Engage your core and keep your shoulder blades retracted. Inhale as you lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, feeling a stretch in your chest, until your arms are parallel to the ground. Exhale as you bring the dumbbells back together over your chest, squeezing your chest muscles. Keep the same slight bend in your elbows throughout and avoid lowering the weights too far, which can strain your shoulders.

name: Dumbbell Shrugs
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides, palms facing your body. Keep your core engaged and shoulders relaxed. Exhale as you shrug your shoulders straight up toward your ears, focusing on contracting your traps. Pause briefly at the top, then inhale as you lower your shoulders back down with control. Keep your arms straight and avoid rolling your shoulders forward or backward. Maintain a neutral neck position to avoid strain.

name: Dumbbell Chest Press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet planted on the ground, holding a dumbbell in each hand at chest level, palms facing forward. Position your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your torso, keeping your shoulder blades retracted. Exhale as you press the dumbbells straight up over your chest, bringing them close together at the top without locking your elbows. Inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to chest level with control, feeling a stretch in your chest. Avoid bouncing the weights off your chest or arching your back.

name: Dumbbell Reverse Snow Angels
instructions: Lie face down on a mat with your arms at your sides, holding light dumbbells in each hand, palms facing up. Engage your core and squeeze your glutes to stabilize your body. Keep your legs on the ground and your neck in a neutral position. Exhale as you slowly lift your chest and arms off the ground, keeping your arms straight. Move your arms out to the sides to form a T-shape with your body, then continue upward to form a Y-shape overhead. As you move, rotate your shoulders so your palms face down at the top of the movement. Inhale as you reverse the motion, bringing your arms back to your sides, then lower your chest and arms to the starting position. Avoid shrugging your shoulders or overarching your lower back.

name: Flat Dumbbell Press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet planted on the ground, holding a dumbbell in each hand at chest level, palms facing forward. Position your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your torso, keeping your shoulder blades retracted. Exhale as you press the dumbbells straight up over your chest, bringing them close together at the top without locking your elbows. Inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to chest level with control, feeling a stretch in your chest. Avoid bouncing the weights off your chest or arching your back.

name: Front Dumbbell Raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand in front of your thighs, palms facing your body. Engage your core and keep your shoulders relaxed. Exhale as you raise the dumbbells straight in front of you to shoulder height, keeping your arms straight with a slight bend in your elbows. Pause briefly at the top, then inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with control. Avoid swinging the weights or leaning back to lift.

name: Front Barbell Raises
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart. Engage your core and keep your shoulders relaxed. Exhale as you raise the barbell straight in front of you to shoulder height, keeping your arms straight with a slight bend in your elbows. Pause briefly at the top, then inhale as you lower the barbell back to the starting position with control. Avoid swinging the bar or leaning back to lift.

name: Glute Bridge Dumbbell
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent, feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart, and a dumbbell resting across your hips (hold it in place with your hands). Engage your core and keep your arms by your sides for stability. Exhale as you press through your heels, lifting your hips toward the ceiling until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees, squeezing your glutes at the top. Inhale as you lower your hips back down with control, stopping just above the ground. Avoid overarching your lower back or letting your knees splay outward.

name: Superman Hold Dumbbells
instructions: Lie face down on a mat with your arms extended in front of you, holding a light dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other. Engage your core and squeeze your glutes to stabilize your body. Exhale as you simultaneously lift your arms, chest, and legs off the ground, keeping your neck neutral and gazing slightly forward. Hold this position for a few seconds, feeling the contraction in your lower back and glutes, then inhale as you lower back down with control. Avoid overarching your back or shrugging your shoulders.

name: Goblet Squat
instructions: Stand with your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out, holding a dumbbell or kettlebell at chest level with both hands, elbows pointing down. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Inhale as you push your hips back and bend your knees, lowering into a squat until your thighs are at least parallel to the ground. Keep your knees tracking over your toes and your back straight. Exhale as you drive through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top. Avoid letting your knees cave inward or your chest collapse forward.

name: Good Morning
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back (on your traps, not your neck), hands gripping the bar just outside your shoulders. Keep a slight bend in your knees and engage your core. Inhale as you hinge at your hips, pushing your hips back and lowering your torso until it’s nearly parallel to the ground, feeling a stretch in your hamstrings. Keep your back flat and neck neutral. Exhale as you drive your hips forward to return to the starting position, squeezing your glutes at the top. Avoid rounding your back or letting your knees bend excessively.

name: Hammer Curl
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides, palms facing your body (neutral grip). Keep your elbows tucked into your torso and your shoulders relaxed. Exhale as you curl the dumbbells toward your shoulders, keeping your wrists neutral and focusing on contracting your biceps and forearms. Pause at the top, then inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with control. Avoid swinging the weights or using your shoulders to lift.

name: Hammer Curls
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides, palms facing your body (neutral grip). Keep your elbows tucked into your torso and your shoulders relaxed. Exhale as you curl the dumbbells toward your shoulders, keeping your wrists neutral and focusing on contracting your biceps and forearms. Pause at the top, then inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with control. Avoid swinging the weights or using your shoulders to lift.

name: Hamstring Curl Machine
instructions: Lie face down on a hamstring curl machine, adjusting the pad so it rests just above your heels, with your knees aligned with the machine’s pivot point. Grasp the handles for stability and keep your hips pressed into the bench. Engage your core and exhale as you curl the pad toward your glutes, squeezing your hamstrings at the top of the movement. Inhale as you slowly lower the pad back to the starting position, maintaining control. Avoid lifting your hips off the bench or arching your back.

name: Hamstring Curls
instructions: Lie face down on a mat with a light dumbbell placed between your feet (or use bodyweight for a lighter variation). Keep your legs straight and engage your core. Exhale as you bend your knees, curling your heels toward your glutes and squeezing your hamstrings at the top. Inhale as you slowly lower your legs back to the starting position, maintaining control. Keep your hips pressed into the ground and avoid arching your back. If using a dumbbell, ensure it’s secure to avoid dropping it.

name: Incline Barbell Press
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 30-45 degree angle and lie back with your feet planted on the ground. Grasp a barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width. Unrack the bar and position it over your upper chest. Inhale to brace your core, then lower the bar to your upper chest, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your torso. Exhale as you press the bar back up to the starting position, focusing on your upper chest and shoulders. Keep your shoulder blades retracted and avoid locking your elbows at the top.

name: Incline Dumbbell Curl
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 45-60 degree angle and sit with your back fully supported, feet flat on the floor. Hold a dumbbell in each hand, arms fully extended, palms facing up, and let your arms hang down behind your body. Keep your elbows close to your torso. Exhale as you curl the dumbbells toward your shoulders, focusing on squeezing your biceps and keeping your upper arms stationary. Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, maintaining tension in your biceps. Avoid swinging the weights or letting your elbows flare out.

name: Incline Dumbbell Press
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 30-45 degree angle and lie back with your feet planted on the ground, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height, palms facing forward. Position your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your torso, keeping your shoulder blades retracted. Exhale as you press the dumbbells straight up over your chest, bringing them close together at the top without locking your elbows. Inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height with control, feeling a stretch in your upper chest. Avoid bouncing the weights or arching your back.

name: Incline Press
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 30-45 degree angle and lie back with your feet planted on the ground, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height, palms facing forward. Position your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your torso, keeping your shoulder blades retracted. Exhale as you press the dumbbells straight up over your chest, bringing them close together at the top without locking your elbows. Inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height with control, feeling a stretch in your upper chest. Avoid bouncing the weights or arching your back.

name: Incline Smith Machine Bench Press
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 30-45 degree angle inside a Smith machine. Lie back on the bench with your feet planted on the ground, and grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width. Unrack the bar by rotating your wrists and position it over your upper chest. Inhale to brace your core, then lower the bar to your upper chest, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your torso. Exhale as you press the bar back up to the starting position, focusing on your upper chest. Avoid locking your elbows at the top or bouncing the bar.

name: Lateral Raise
instructions: Stand tall with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides, palms facing in. Engage your core and keep your shoulders relaxed. Exhale as you raise your arms out to the sides to shoulder height, keeping a slight bend in your elbows to avoid strain. Pause briefly at the top, then inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to your sides with control. Avoid swinging the weights or shrugging your shoulders toward your ears.

name: Lateral Raises
instructions: Stand tall with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides, palms facing in. Engage your core and keep your shoulders relaxed. Exhale as you raise your arms out to the sides to shoulder height, keeping a slight bend in your elbows to avoid strain. Pause briefly at the top, then inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to your sides with control. Avoid swinging the weights or shrugging your shoulders toward your ears.

name: Leg Extension
instructions: Sit on a leg extension machine with your back against the pad and your shins under the padded bar, adjusting the machine so your knees align with the pivot point. Grasp the handles for stability and engage your core. Exhale as you extend your legs until they are almost straight, focusing on contracting your quads, but avoid locking your knees. Inhale as you slowly lower the weight back to the starting position, keeping the movement controlled. Keep your back against the pad and avoid using momentum to lift the weight.

name: Leg Press
instructions: Sit on a leg press machine with your back and hips against the seat, feet shoulder-width apart on the platform, toes slightly turned out. Adjust the seat so your knees are at a 90-degree angle. Grasp the handles for stability and engage your core. Exhale as you press through your heels to extend your legs, stopping just before locking your knees. Inhale as you slowly lower the platform back down until your knees are at a 90-degree angle again. Keep your hips and back against the seat and avoid rounding your lower back.

name: Leg Press Calve Raises
instructions: Sit on a leg press machine with your back against the seat, placing the balls of your feet on the lower edge of the platform, heels hanging off, and legs straight but not locked. Grasp the handles for stability. Exhale as you press the platform away by pointing your toes, flexing your calves at the top of the movement. Inhale as you lower the platform by letting your heels drop slightly, feeling a stretch in your calves. Keep the movement controlled and avoid bouncing.

name: Machine Shoulder Press
instructions: Sit on a shoulder press machine with your back against the pad, feet flat on the floor, and hands grasping the handles at shoulder height, palms facing forward. Adjust the seat so the handles are at shoulder level. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Exhale as you press the handles upward until your arms are fully extended but not locked out. Inhale as you lower the handles back to the starting position with control. Avoid shrugging your shoulders or arching your back during the movement.

name: EZ Bar Front Raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding an EZ bar in front of your thighs with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart. Engage your core and keep your shoulders relaxed. Exhale as you raise the bar straight in front of you to shoulder height, keeping your arms straight with a slight bend in your elbows. Pause briefly at the top, then inhale as you lower the bar back to the starting position with control. Avoid swinging the bar or leaning back to lift.

name: Overhead Dumbbell Extension
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the floor, holding a dumbbell with both hands by gripping the handle, palms facing up. Lift the dumbbell overhead so your arms are fully extended, keeping your elbows close to your head. Engage your core to avoid arching your back. Inhale as you lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary. Lower until your forearms are parallel to the ground, then exhale as you extend your arms back to the starting position, fully contracting your triceps. Avoid flaring your elbows out or locking your elbows at the top.

name: Plank
instructions: Start in a forearm plank position with your elbows directly under your shoulders, forearms on the ground, and body in a straight line from head to heels. Engage your core by pulling your belly button toward your spine, and squeeze your glutes to stabilize your hips. Keep your neck neutral by looking at the floor just ahead of you. Hold this position, breathing steadily, without letting your hips sag or pike up. For a regression, drop to your knees while keeping your body straight.

name: Preacher Curl
instructions: Sit on a preacher curl bench with your upper arms resting on the pad, holding an EZ bar or barbell with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart. Adjust the seat so your armpits are just above the pad. Engage your core and keep your shoulders relaxed. Exhale as you curl the bar toward your shoulders, focusing on squeezing your biceps and keeping your upper arms pressed into the pad. Pause at the top, then inhale as you slowly lower the bar back to the starting position, maintaining tension in your biceps. Avoid using your shoulders or back to lift the weight.

name: Pull Ups
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, arms fully extended. Engage your core and squeeze your glutes to stabilize your body. Pull your shoulder blades down and back to initiate the movement. Exhale as you pull your body up, driving your elbows down until your chin is above the bar and your lats are fully contracted. Inhale as you slowly lower yourself back to the starting position, maintaining control. Avoid swinging your body or kipping to use momentum.

name: Push Ups
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands shoulder-width apart, directly under your shoulders, and body in a straight line from head to heels. Engage your core and squeeze your glutes to stabilize your body. Inhale as you lower your chest toward the ground, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your torso. Exhale as you push back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms. Avoid letting your hips sag or your shoulders shrug. For a regression, drop to your knees while keeping your body straight.

name: Reverse Fly Dumbbells
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other. Hinge at your hips to lean forward slightly, keeping your back flat and core engaged, with the dumbbells hanging below your chest. Exhale as you raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, squeezing your rear delts and upper back at the top, until your arms are parallel to the ground. Inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with control. Keep a slight bend in your elbows and avoid shrugging your shoulders.

name: Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart. Keep a slight bend in your knees and engage your core. Inhale as you hinge at your hips, pushing your hips back and lowering the barbell toward the ground, keeping it close to your legs. Lower until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings, keeping your back flat and chest up. Exhale as you drive through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top. Avoid rounding your back or letting the bar drift forward.

name: Romanian Deadlift Dumbbells
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand in front of your thighs, palms facing your body. Keep a slight bend in your knees and engage your core. Inhale as you hinge at your hips, pushing your hips back and lowering the dumbbells toward the ground, keeping them close to your legs. Lower until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings, keeping your back flat and chest up. Exhale as you drive through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top. Avoid rounding your back or letting the dumbbells drift forward.

name: Seated Cable Row
instructions: Sit at a cable row machine with your feet on the footrests, knees slightly bent, and grasp the handle (V-bar or wide bar) with both hands. Sit upright with your back straight and engage your core. Exhale as you pull the handle toward your lower chest, driving your elbows back and squeezing your shoulder blades together. Keep your torso stationary and avoid leaning back excessively. Inhale as you slowly extend your arms back to the starting position, maintaining control. Avoid shrugging your shoulders or using your biceps to pull.

name: Seated Dumbbell Bicep Curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the floor, holding a dumbbell in each hand, arms by your sides, palms facing forward. Keep your back straight and elbows tucked into your torso. Exhale as you curl the dumbbells toward your shoulders, focusing on squeezing your biceps and keeping your upper arms stationary. Pause at the top, then inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, maintaining control. Avoid swinging the weights or using your shoulders to lift.

name: Seated Dumbbell Row
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the floor, holding a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other. Lean forward slightly at your hips, keeping your back flat and core engaged, with the dumbbells hanging below your chest. Exhale as you row the dumbbells up toward your waist, driving your elbows back and squeezing your upper back. Inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with control. Avoid shrugging your shoulders or using your biceps excessively.

name: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 90-degree angle and sit with your back fully supported, feet flat on the floor. Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height, palms facing forward, elbows bent at 90 degrees and pointing downward. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Exhale as you press the dumbbells overhead, bringing them close together at the top without locking your elbows. Inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your torso. Avoid shrugging your shoulders or arching your back during the movement.

name: Seated Leg Press
instructions: Sit on a seated leg press machine with your back against the pad, feet shoulder-width apart on the platform, toes slightly turned out. Adjust the seat so your knees are at a 90-degree angle. Grasp the handles for stability and engage your core. Exhale as you press the platform away, extending your legs until they are almost straight, but avoid locking your knees. Inhale as you slowly lower the platform back to the starting position, keeping your hips and back against the seat. Avoid rounding your lower back or lifting your hips off the seat.

name: Seated Machine Chest Press
instructions: Sit on a chest press machine with your back against the pad, feet flat on the floor, and hands grasping the handles at chest height, palms facing forward. Adjust the seat so the handles are at mid-chest level. Engage your core and keep your shoulder blades retracted. Exhale as you press the handles forward until your arms are fully extended but not locked out. Inhale as you slowly return the handles to the starting position, feeling a stretch in your chest. Avoid shrugging your shoulders or arching your back during the movement.

name: Shoulder Press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height, palms facing forward, elbows bent at 90 degrees and pointing downward. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Exhale as you press the dumbbells overhead, bringing them close together at the top without locking your elbows. Inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your torso. Avoid shrugging your shoulders or leaning back excessively.

name: Shrugs
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart. Keep your core engaged and shoulders relaxed. Exhale as you shrug your shoulders straight up toward your ears, focusing on contracting your traps. Pause briefly at the top, then inhale as you lower your shoulders back down with control. Keep your arms straight and avoid rolling your shoulders forward or backward. Maintain a neutral neck position to avoid strain.

name: Single Arm Cable Chest Press
instructions: Set a cable machine pulley to chest height. Stand in a staggered stance (one foot slightly ahead), facing away from the machine, and grab the handle with your right hand, palm facing down. Position your right arm at a 90-degree angle, elbow in line with your shoulder, and keep your left hand on your hip for stability. Engage your core and maintain a slight forward lean. Exhale as you press the handle forward, fully extending your arm and contracting your chest. Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position, keeping the movement controlled. Repeat on the other side. Avoid rotating your torso or shrugging your shoulder.

name: Single Arm Dumbbell Chest Press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet planted on the ground, holding a dumbbell in your right hand at chest level, palm facing forward, and your left hand resting on your hip or holding the bench for stability. Position your right elbow at a 45-degree angle to your torso, keeping your shoulder blades retracted. Exhale as you press the dumbbell straight up over your chest, fully extending your arm but not locking your elbow. Inhale as you lower the dumbbell back to chest level with control, feeling a stretch in your chest. Repeat on the other side. Avoid rotating your torso or arching your back.

name: Single Arm Overhand Extension
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in your right hand. Extend your right arm overhead, keeping your elbow close to your head and palm facing forward. Place your left hand on your hip for stability. Inhale as you bend your right elbow, lowering the dumbbell behind your head until your forearm is parallel to the ground. Exhale as you extend your arm back to the starting position, fully contracting your triceps. Keep your upper arm stationary and avoid locking your elbow at the top. Repeat on the other side.

name: Smith Machine Squat
instructions: Position a Smith machine bar at a height just below your shoulders. Step under the bar and place it across your upper back (on your traps), gripping the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width. Unrack the bar by rotating your wrists and step forward slightly, setting your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out. Inhale to brace your core, then lower into a squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees until your thighs are at least parallel to the ground. Exhale as you drive through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top. Keep your back neutral and avoid letting your knees cave inward.

name: Standing Calf Raises
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, either on the floor or on a step with your heels hanging off the edge for greater range of motion. Hold onto a wall or sturdy surface for balance if needed. Engage your core and keep your shoulders relaxed. Exhale as you rise onto the balls of your feet, squeezing your calves at the top of the movement. Pause briefly, then inhale as you lower your heels back down with control, feeling a stretch in your calves. Avoid bouncing or using momentum.

name: Superman Hold
instructions: Lie face down on a mat with your arms extended in front of you, legs straight, and palms facing down. Engage your core and squeeze your glutes to stabilize your body. Exhale as you simultaneously lift your arms, chest, and legs off the ground, keeping your neck neutral and gazing slightly forward. Hold this position for a few seconds, feeling the contraction in your lower back and glutes, then inhale as you lower back down with control. Avoid overarching your back or shrugging your shoulders.

name: Towel Row
instructions: Wrap a towel around a sturdy vertical pole or door frame, ensuring it’s secure. Stand facing the pole, holding each end of the towel with your hands, palms facing each other. Lean back slightly with your arms extended, keeping your body in a straight line from head to heels and your core engaged. Exhale as you pull your chest toward the pole by bending your elbows, squeezing your lats and upper back. Inhale as you slowly extend your arms to return to the starting position, maintaining control. Avoid shrugging your shoulders or letting your hips sag.

name: Tricep Dips
instructions: Position yourself on parallel bars with your arms straight, hands gripping the bars, and feet hanging or slightly bent at the knees. Keep your torso upright and engage your core. Inhale as you bend your elbows to lower your body, stopping when your upper arms are parallel to the ground or you feel a stretch in your shoulders. Exhale as you press through your hands to extend your arms and return to the starting position, focusing on your triceps. Avoid flaring your elbows out excessively or letting your shoulders shrug.

name: Tricep Pushdown Rope
instructions: Set a cable machine with a rope attachment at the highest setting. Stand facing the machine, feet shoulder-width apart, and grab the rope with both hands, palms facing each other. Pin your elbows to your sides, keeping your upper arms stationary. Exhale as you push the rope down, separating the ends as you fully extend your arms, focusing on contracting your triceps. Inhale as you slowly return the rope to chest height, maintaining control. Avoid leaning forward or using your shoulders to push the weight.

name: Wide Grip Lat Pull Down
instructions: Sit at a lat pull-down machine and adjust the seat so your thighs are secured under the pads. Grasp the wide bar with an overhand grip, hands wider than shoulder-width apart. Sit upright, feet flat on the floor, and engage your core. Lean back slightly (about 20 degrees) and pull your shoulder blades down and back. Exhale as you pull the bar down to your upper chest, driving your elbows down and squeezing your lats. Pause briefly, then inhale as you return the bar to the starting position with control. Avoid arching your back or using momentum to pull the weight.

name: Incline Push Ups
instructions: Place your hands on an elevated surface like a bench or box, shoulder-width apart, with your body in a straight line from head to heels. Engage your core and keep your glutes tight. Inhale as you lower your chest toward the surface, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your torso. Exhale as you push back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms. Avoid letting your hips sag or your shoulders shrug. This is a regression from standard push-ups, making it easier on the upper body.

name: Inverted Row
instructions: Set a barbell in a rack at about waist height or use a Smith machine bar. Lie on your back under the bar, grasping it with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart. Extend your legs straight or keep your knees bent with feet flat on the floor for an easier variation. Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels, core engaged. Exhale as you pull your chest toward the bar, driving your elbows back and squeezing your shoulder blades together. Inhale as you lower back down with control. Avoid shrugging your shoulders or letting your hips sag.

name: Cable Bicep Curl
instructions: Set a cable machine with a straight bar attachment at the lowest setting. Stand facing the machine, feet shoulder-width apart, and grasp the bar with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart. Keep your elbows tucked into your sides and your torso upright. Exhale as you curl the bar toward your chest, focusing on squeezing your biceps and keeping your upper arms stationary. Pause at the top, then inhale as you slowly lower the bar back to the starting position, maintaining tension in your biceps. Avoid swinging your body or letting your elbows flare out.

name: Hip Thrust
instructions: Sit on the floor with your upper back against a bench, knees bent, and feet flat on the ground, hip-width apart. Place your hands on your hips or the bench for stability. Engage your core and keep your chin tucked. Exhale as you drive through your heels, lifting your hips until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees, squeezing your glutes at the top. Inhale as you lower your hips back down with control, stopping just above the ground. Avoid overarching your lower back or letting your knees cave inward.

name: Glute Bridge
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent, feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart, and arms by your sides. Engage your core and keep your spine neutral. Exhale as you press through your heels, lifting your hips toward the ceiling until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees, squeezing your glutes at the top. Inhale as you lower your hips back down with control, stopping just above the ground. Avoid overarching your lower back or letting your knees splay outward.

name: Dumbbell Goblet Squat
instructions: Stand with your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out, holding a dumbbell at chest level with both hands, elbows pointing down. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Inhale as you push your hips back and bend your knees, lowering into a squat until your thighs are at least parallel to the ground. Keep your knees tracking over your toes and your back straight. Exhale as you drive through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top. Avoid letting your knees cave inward or your chest collapse forward.

name: Dumbbell Pullover
instructions: Lie perpendicular on a flat bench with only your upper back and shoulders on the bench, feet flat on the floor, and hips lowered toward the ground. Hold a dumbbell with both hands above your chest, gripping the handle, palms facing up, with a slight bend in your elbows. Engage your core to keep your hips stable. Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbell back over your head in an arc, feeling a stretch in your lats and chest. Lower until your arms are parallel to the floor, then exhale as you pull the dumbbell back to the starting position, squeezing your lats and chest. Keep your hips steady and avoid overarching your lower back.

name: Side Plank
instructions: Lie on your right side with your legs straight and feet stacked. Prop yourself up on your right forearm, elbow directly under your shoulder, and engage your core. Lift your hips off the ground so your body forms a straight line from head to heels, keeping your left hand on your hip or raised toward the ceiling for balance. Hold this position, breathing steadily, without letting your hips sag. Repeat on the left side. For a regression, bend your knees and keep them on the ground while lifting your hips.

name: Bicycle Crunches
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent, feet off the floor, and hands lightly behind your ears. Engage your core by pulling your belly button toward your spine. Exhale as you lift your shoulder blades off the ground and bring your right elbow toward your left knee, extending your right leg out straight. Alternate sides in a pedaling motion, bringing your left elbow toward your right knee while extending your left leg. Keep your movements controlled and avoid pulling on your neck. Continue alternating for the desired number of reps.

name: Cat-Cow Stretch
instructions: Start on your hands and knees in a tabletop position, with your wrists under your shoulders and knees under your hips. Inhale as you drop your belly toward the floor, lifting your chest and tailbone toward the ceiling (Cow position), and look slightly forward. Exhale as you round your back, tucking your chin to your chest and pulling your belly button toward your spine (Cat position). Move slowly between these two positions, syncing your breath with the movement, to warm up your spine. Avoid forcing the stretch or overarching your back excessively.

name: Chair Squats
instructions: Stand in front of a sturdy chair with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Inhale as you push your hips back and bend your knees, lowering into a squat until your glutes lightly touch the chair. Keep your knees tracking over your toes and your back neutral. Exhale as you drive through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top. Avoid letting your knees cave inward or your back round. This is a regression from bodyweight squats, providing support for beginners.

name: Jump Rope
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a jump rope with a handle in each hand, elbows close to your sides. Engage your core and keep your shoulders relaxed. Begin swinging the rope over your head and jump lightly off the ground as the rope passes under your feet, landing softly on the balls of your feet. Maintain a steady rhythm, keeping your jumps low to the ground and your wrists driving the rope’s motion. Breathe steadily and avoid hunching your shoulders or over-jumping.

name: Standing Arm Swings
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, arms relaxed by your sides. Engage your core and keep your shoulders relaxed. Begin swinging your arms forward and backward in a controlled motion, gradually increasing the range of motion to warm up your shoulders. After 15 seconds, switch to crossing your arms in front of your chest and then opening them wide to the sides, feeling a stretch in your chest and shoulders. Keep your movements smooth and avoid shrugging your shoulders. Perform for the desired duration to warm up your upper body.

name: Walking Lunges
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, hands on your hips or holding dumbbells by your sides for added resistance. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Step forward with your right leg, lowering your hips until both knees are bent at about 90 degrees, with your left knee hovering just above the ground. Keep your front knee over your ankle and your back straight. Exhale as you push through your right heel to step forward with your left leg, bringing your feet together, then repeat on the other side. Continue walking forward, alternating legs. Avoid letting your front knee collapse inward or your torso lean forward excessively.

name: Dumbbell Lunges
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides, palms facing your body. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Step forward with your right leg, lowering your hips until both knees are bent at about 90 degrees, with your left knee hovering just above the ground. Keep your front knee over your ankle and your back straight. Exhale as you push through your right heel to return to the starting position, then repeat on the left leg. Alternate legs for the desired number of reps. Avoid letting your front knee collapse inward or your torso lean forward excessively.

name: Russian Twists
instructions: Sit on the floor with your knees bent, feet flat on the ground, and lean back slightly to engage your core, keeping your back straight. Hold a dumbbell or weight plate with both hands at chest level, or use just your bodyweight for a lighter variation. Lift your feet off the ground (or keep them grounded for an easier version) and balance on your sit bones. Exhale as you twist your torso to the right, bringing the weight toward the floor beside your hip, then inhale as you twist to the left. Continue alternating sides in a controlled motion, keeping your core engaged. Avoid rounding your back or using momentum to twist.

name: Leg Raises
instructions: Lie on your back on a mat with your legs straight, arms by your sides or under your glutes for support, palms facing down. Engage your core by pulling your belly button toward your spine and pressing your lower back into the floor. Exhale as you lift your legs straight up until they’re perpendicular to the floor, keeping them together. Inhale as you slowly lower your legs back down, stopping just above the ground without letting your lower back arch. Keep the movement controlled and avoid using momentum to lift your legs.

name: Mountain Climbers
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands shoulder-width apart, directly under your shoulders, and body in a straight line from head to heels. Engage your core and keep your hips level. Quickly drive your right knee toward your chest, then as you extend it back, drive your left knee toward your chest, alternating legs in a running motion. Keep your movements fast and controlled, maintaining a strong plank position. Breathe steadily and avoid letting your hips pike up or sag.

name: Step-Ups
instructions: Stand in front of a sturdy bench or box (knee height or slightly higher), holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides or using just your bodyweight. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Step onto the bench with your right foot, pressing through your heel to lift your body up, bringing your left foot to meet your right on the bench. Step back down with your left foot, then your right, returning to the starting position. Repeat, leading with the same leg for the set, then switch sides. Keep your movements controlled and avoid bouncing or using momentum.

name: Dumbbell Step-Ups
instructions: Stand in front of a sturdy bench or box (knee height or slightly higher), holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides, palms facing your body. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Step onto the bench with your right foot, pressing through your heel to lift your body up, bringing your left foot to meet your right on the bench. Step back down with your left foot, then your right, returning to the starting position. Repeat, leading with the same leg for the set, then switch sides. Keep your movements controlled and avoid bouncing or using momentum.

name: Kettlebell Swing
instructions: Stand with your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell with both hands in front of your thighs, palms facing your body. Engage your core and keep a slight bend in your knees. Inhale as you hinge at your hips, swinging the kettlebell back between your legs, keeping your back flat. Exhale as you drive your hips forward, swinging the kettlebell up to chest height, using the momentum from your hips, not your arms. Let the kettlebell swing back down as you hinge again, maintaining a smooth rhythm. Avoid rounding your back or squatting too deeply.

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
instructions: Stand about two feet in front of a bench or box, holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides or using just your bodyweight. Place your left foot on the bench behind you, laces down, and keep your right foot flat on the ground. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Inhale as you bend your right knee, lowering your hips until your right thigh is parallel to the ground, keeping your front knee over your ankle. Exhale as you push through your right heel to return to the starting position. Complete the set on one side, then switch legs. Avoid letting your front knee collapse inward or your torso lean forward excessively.

name: Dumbbell Bulgarian Split Squat
instructions: Stand about two feet in front of a bench or box, holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides, palms facing your body. Place your left foot on the bench behind you, laces down, and keep your right foot flat on the ground. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Inhale as you bend your right knee, lowering your hips until your right thigh is parallel to the ground, keeping your front knee over your ankle. Exhale as you push through your right heel to return to the starting position. Complete the set on one side, then switch legs. Avoid letting your front knee collapse inward or your torso lean forward excessively.

name: Side Lateral Raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides, palms facing in. Engage your core and keep your shoulders relaxed. Exhale as you raise your arms out to the sides to shoulder height, keeping a slight bend in your elbows to avoid strain. Pause briefly at the top, then inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to your sides with control. Avoid swinging the weights or shrugging your shoulders toward your ears.

name: Cable Lateral Raise
instructions: Set a cable machine pulley to the lowest setting and attach a single handle. Stand with your left side to the machine, holding the handle in your right hand, palm facing in. Step away slightly to create tension, keeping your feet shoulder-width apart and core engaged. Exhale as you raise your right arm out to the side to shoulder height, keeping a slight bend in your elbow. Pause briefly at the top, then inhale as you lower the handle back to the starting position with control. Repeat on the other side. Avoid swinging the weight or shrugging your shoulder.

name: Bent-Over Row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with an overhand grip, hands just outside your knees. Hinge at your hips to lean forward, keeping your back flat and core engaged, until your torso is nearly parallel to the ground. Let the barbell hang straight down. Exhale as you row the barbell up toward your lower chest, driving your elbows back and squeezing your shoulder blades together. Inhale as you lower the barbell back to the starting position with control. Avoid rounding your back or using momentum to lift the weight.

name: Dumbbell Bent-Over Row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other. Hinge at your hips to lean forward, keeping your back flat and core engaged, until your torso is nearly parallel to the ground. Let the dumbbells hang straight down. Exhale as you row the dumbbells up toward your waist, driving your elbows back and squeezing your shoulder blades together. Inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with control. Avoid rounding your back or using momentum to lift the weights.

name: Squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out, holding a barbell across your upper back (on your traps) or using just your bodyweight. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Inhale as you push your hips back and bend your knees, lowering into a squat until your thighs are at least parallel to the ground. Keep your knees tracking over your toes and your back neutral. Exhale as you drive through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top. Avoid letting your knees cave inward or your back round.

name: Front Squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell in a front rack position (resting on your shoulders, elbows high, hands lightly gripping the bar). Engage your core and keep your chest up. Inhale as you push your hips back and bend your knees, lowering into a squat until your thighs are at least parallel to the ground. Keep your elbows high, knees tracking over your toes, and back straight. Exhale as you drive through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top. Avoid letting your elbows drop or your back round.

name: Overhead Squat
