instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell overhead with a wide grip, arms fully extended, and shoulders engaged. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Inhale as you push your hips back and bend your knees, lowering into a squat while keeping the barbell directly over your midfoot. Lower until your thighs are at least parallel to the ground, keeping your knees tracking over your toes and back straight. Exhale as you drive through your heels to stand back up, maintaining the barbell overhead. Avoid letting the bar drift forward or your back round.

name: Sumo Deadlift
instructions: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointed out at 45 degrees, and a barbell on the ground in front of you. Bend at your hips and knees to grasp the bar with a mixed or overhand grip, hands inside your knees. Keep your back flat, chest up, and shoulders slightly in front of the bar. Engage your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back. Inhale to brace, then exhale as you lift the bar by driving through your heels and extending your hips and knees, keeping the bar close to your body. Stand tall, squeezing your glutes at the top, then inhale as you lower the bar back to the ground with control. Avoid rounding your back or jerking the bar off the ground.

name: Stiff-Leg Deadlift
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart. Keep your knees slightly bent and engage your core. Inhale as you hinge at your hips, lowering the barbell toward the ground while keeping your legs mostly straight, feeling a stretch in your hamstrings. Keep your back flat and the bar close to your legs. Exhale as you drive your hips forward to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top. Avoid rounding your back or letting the bar drift forward.

name: Dumbbell Stiff-Leg Deadlift
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand in front of your thighs, palms facing your body. Keep your knees slightly bent and engage your core. Inhale as you hinge at your hips, lowering the dumbbells toward the ground while keeping your legs mostly straight, feeling a stretch in your hamstrings. Keep your back flat and the dumbbells close to your legs. Exhale as you drive your hips forward to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top. Avoid rounding your back or letting the dumbbells drift forward.

name: Push Press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell at shoulder height with an overhand grip, hands just outside your shoulders. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Slightly bend your knees to dip down, then explosively drive through your legs, using the momentum to press the barbell overhead, fully extending your arms. At the top, shrug your shoulders slightly to engage your traps, ensuring the bar is balanced over your midfoot. Inhale as you lower the bar back to your shoulders, absorbing the weight with a slight knee bend. Avoid arching your lower back or leaning back excessively.

name: Arnold Press
instructions: Sit on a bench with a backrest or stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height, palms facing your body, elbows bent. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Exhale as you press the dumbbells overhead, rotating your palms to face forward as you extend your arms, fully extending but not locking your elbows. Inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, rotating your palms back to face your body. Avoid shrugging your shoulders or arching your back during the movement.

name: Incline Dumbbell Fly
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 30-45 degree angle and lie back with your feet planted on the ground, holding a dumbbell in each hand above your chest, palms facing each other, elbows slightly bent. Engage your core and keep your shoulder blades retracted. Inhale as you lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, feeling a stretch in your upper chest, until your arms are parallel to the ground. Exhale as you bring the dumbbells back together over your chest, squeezing your chest muscles. Keep the same slight bend in your elbows throughout and avoid lowering the weights too far, which can strain your shoulders.

name: 12line Dumbbell Press
instructions: Lie on a 12line bench with your feet secured, holding a dumbbell in each hand at chest level, palms facing forward. Position your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your torso, keeping your shoulder blades retracted. Exhale as you press the dumbbells straight up over your lower chest, bringing them close together at the top without locking your elbows. Inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to chest level with control, feeling a stretch in your lower chest. Avoid bouncing the weights or arching your back.

name: Cable Tricep Kickback
instructions: Set a cable machine pulley to the lowest setting and attach a single handle. Stand facing the machine, hinge at your hips to lean forward slightly, and grab the handle with your right hand, palm facing in. Pin your right elbow to your side, keeping your upper arm parallel to the ground. Exhale as you extend your arm back, fully straightening it and contracting your triceps. Inhale as you slowly return the handle to the starting position, keeping your elbow stationary. Repeat on the other side. Avoid swinging the weight or using your shoulder to move the cable.

name: Dumbbell Tricep Kickback
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing in. Hinge at your hips to lean forward slightly, keeping your back flat and core engaged. Bend your elbows to bring the dumbbells up to your sides, keeping your upper arms parallel to the ground. Exhale as you extend your arms back, fully straightening them and contracting your triceps. Inhale as you return to the starting position, keeping your elbows stationary. Avoid swinging the weights or using your shoulders to move the dumbbells.

name: Concentration Curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the floor, holding a dumbbell in your right hand. Rest your right elbow on the inside of your right thigh, letting the dumbbell hang down toward the floor, palm facing up. Keep your back straight and core engaged. Exhale as you curl the dumbbell toward your shoulder, focusing on squeezing your biceps and keeping your upper arm stationary. Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position, maintaining control. Repeat on the other side. Avoid using your shoulder or torso to lift the weight.

name: Cable Woodchopper
instructions: Set a cable machine pulley to the highest setting and attach a single handle. Stand with your left side to the machine, feet shoulder-width apart, and grab the handle with both hands, arms extended. Engage your core and keep your knees slightly bent. Exhale as you pull the handle down and across your body toward your right hip, rotating your torso and pivoting your left foot, focusing on your obliques. Inhale as you return to the starting position with control. Repeat on the other side. Avoid using your arms alone to pull the weight; focus on the rotation of your torso.

name: Hanging Leg Raise
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart, arms fully extended, and legs straight. Engage your core and keep your body stable. Exhale as you lift your legs straight up in front of you until they’re parallel to the ground (or higher if possible), keeping your legs together. Inhale as you slowly lower your legs back to the starting position with control, avoiding swinging. For a regression, bend your knees and lift them toward your chest instead. Keep your movements controlled and avoid using momentum.

name: Bird Dog
instructions: Start on your hands and knees in a tabletop position, with your wrists under your shoulders and knees under your hips. Engage your core to keep your spine neutral. Exhale as you extend your right arm forward and your left leg back, keeping both straight and in line with your torso, balancing on your left hand and right knee. Hold briefly, then inhale as you return to the starting position. Repeat on the other side, extending your left arm and right leg. Keep your movements controlled and avoid arching your back or rotating your hips.

name: High Knees
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, arms bent at 90 degrees by your sides. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Begin jogging in place, lifting your knees as high as possible toward your chest, alternating legs quickly. Pump your arms in sync with your legs, keeping your movements fast and controlled. Land softly on the balls of your feet and maintain a steady rhythm, breathing steadily. Avoid hunching your shoulders or leaning back.

name: Burpees
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, arms by your sides. Squat down and place your hands on the ground in front of you, shoulder-width apart. Jump your feet back into a high plank position, keeping your body in a straight line. Perform a push-up by lowering your chest to the ground, then pushing back up (optional for beginners). Jump your feet back toward your hands, then explosively jump into the air, reaching your arms overhead. Land softly and immediately go into the next rep. Keep your core engaged and avoid letting your hips sag during the plank.

name: Box Jump
instructions: Stand in front of a sturdy box or platform (knee height or higher based on your ability), feet shoulder-width apart. Engage your core and slightly bend your knees. Swing your arms back, then explosively jump onto the box, driving through your heels and swinging your arms forward for momentum. Land softly on the box with both feet, knees slightly bent, and stand up fully. Step or jump back down to the starting position and repeat. Avoid landing with locked knees or letting your knees cave inward.

name: Farmer's Carry
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a heavy dumbbell or kettlebell in each hand by your sides, palms facing in. Engage your core, pull your shoulders back, and keep your chest up. Walk forward in a straight line, taking small, controlled steps, keeping your torso upright and avoiding leaning to one side. Continue for the desired distance or time, then set the weights down with control. Avoid shrugging your shoulders or letting your back round during the carry.

name: Overhead Squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell overhead with a wide grip, arms fully extended, and shoulders engaged. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Inhale as you push your hips back and bend your knees, lowering into a squat while keeping the barbell directly over your midfoot. Lower until your thighs are at least parallel to the ground, keeping your knees tracking over your toes and back straight. Exhale as you drive through your heels to stand back up, maintaining the barbell overhead. Avoid letting the bar drift forward or your back round.

name: Single-Leg Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in your right hand, palm facing your body. Shift your weight onto your left leg, keeping a slight bend in your left knee. Engage your core and keep your back flat. Inhale as you hinge at your hips, extending your right leg straight back while lowering the dumbbell toward the ground, keeping it close to your left leg. Lower until you feel a stretch in your left hamstring, keeping your hips square. Exhale as you drive through your left heel to return to the starting position, squeezing your glutes at the top. Repeat on the other side. Avoid rounding your back or letting your hips rotate.

name: Reverse Lunges
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, hands on your hips or holding dumbbells by your sides for added resistance. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Step backward with your right leg, lowering your hips until both knees are bent at about 90 degrees, with your left knee over your ankle and your right knee hovering just above the ground. Keep your back straight and torso upright. Exhale as you push through your left heel to step your right leg back to the starting position, then repeat on the other side. Avoid letting your front knee collapse inward or your torso lean forward excessively.

name: Side Lunges
instructions: Stand with your feet together, hands on your hips or holding a dumbbell at chest level for added resistance. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Step out to the right with your right leg, bending your right knee and pushing your hips back, keeping your left leg straight and left foot flat on the ground. Lower until your right thigh is parallel to the ground, keeping your right knee over your ankle. Exhale as you push through your right heel to return to the starting position, then repeat on the left side. Avoid letting your knee collapse inward or your torso lean forward excessively.

name: Lateral Step-Ups
instructions: Stand with your left side facing a sturdy bench or box (knee height or slightly higher), holding a dumbbell in your right hand by your side or using just your bodyweight. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Step onto the bench with your left foot, pressing through your heel to lift your body up, bringing your right foot to meet your left on the bench. Step back down with your right foot, then your left, returning to the starting position. Repeat, leading with the same leg for the set, then switch sides. Keep your movements controlled and avoid bouncing or using momentum.

name: Cable Hip Abduction
instructions: Set a cable machine pulley to the lowest setting and attach an ankle strap to your right ankle. Stand with your left side facing the machine, holding onto the machine for balance, and keep your left leg slightly bent. Engage your core and keep your torso upright. Exhale as you lift your right leg out to the side, keeping it straight and focusing on your hip abductors, until your leg is at about a 45-degree angle. Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position with control. Repeat on the other side. Avoid swinging your leg or leaning your torso to the side.

name: Side-Lying Leg Raises
instructions: Lie on your right side with your legs straight and stacked, propping yourself up on your right forearm or resting your head on your arm. Place your left hand on your hip for stability. Engage your core to keep your hips stable. Exhale as you lift your left leg straight up toward the ceiling, focusing on your hip abductors, keeping your toes facing forward. Lift until your leg is at about a 45-degree angle, then inhale as you lower it back down with control, stopping just above your right leg. Repeat on the other side. Avoid rotating your hips or lifting your leg too high, which can strain your lower back.

name: Wrist Curls
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the floor, holding a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing up. Rest your forearms on your thighs, letting your wrists hang off the edge. Engage your core and keep your shoulders relaxed. Exhale as you curl the dumbbells upward by flexing your wrists, focusing on contracting your forearms. Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, feeling a stretch in your forearms. Avoid using your arms or shoulders to lift the weight.

name: Reverse Wrist Curls
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the floor, holding a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing down. Rest your forearms on your thighs, letting your wrists hang off the edge. Engage your core and keep your shoulders relaxed. Exhale as you lift the dumbbells upward by extending your wrists, focusing on contracting the top of your forearms. Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, feeling a stretch in your forearms. Avoid using your arms or shoulders to lift the weight.

name: Rack Pulls
instructions: Set a barbell in a rack just below your knees. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes under the bar, and grasp the bar with an overhand or mixed grip, hands shoulder-width apart. Keep your back flat, chest up, and shoulders slightly in front of the bar. Engage your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back. Inhale to brace, then exhale as you lift the bar by extending your hips and knees, standing tall and squeezing your traps and glutes at the top. Inhale as you lower the bar back to the rack with control. Avoid rounding your back or jerking the bar off the rack.

name: Dragon Flags
instructions: Lie on your back on a bench or the floor, holding onto the bench behind your head or a sturdy object for support. Engage your core and lift your legs and hips off the ground, bringing your feet toward the ceiling so your body is in a straight line, with only your upper back and shoulders on the bench. Inhale as you slowly lower your legs and hips toward the ground in a controlled motion, keeping your body straight and avoiding bending at the hips. Stop just above the ground, then exhale as you lift back to the starting position. Avoid letting your lower back arch or using momentum to lift.

name: Ab Rollouts
instructions: Kneel on a mat with an ab wheel in front of you, holding the handles with both hands. Engage your core and keep your back straight. Exhale as you roll the wheel forward, extending your body into a straight line, lowering your torso toward the ground while keeping your arms straight. Go as far as you can without letting your hips sag or your lower back arch. Inhale as you pull the wheel back toward your knees by contracting your abs, returning to the starting position. Avoid using your arms to pull the wheel; focus on your core.

name: Pallof Press
instructions: Set a cable machine pulley to chest height and attach a single handle. Stand with your left side facing the machine, feet shoulder-width apart, and grab the handle with both hands at chest level. Step away to create tension, keeping your knees slightly bent and core engaged. Exhale as you press the handle straight out in front of you, resisting the rotation of the cable pulling you to the left, and hold for a moment. Inhale as you return the handle to your chest with control. Repeat on the other side. Avoid rotating your torso or hips during the movement.

name: Battle Ropes
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, holding a battle rope in each hand, palms facing in. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Begin alternating your arms to create waves with the ropes, slamming them down forcefully while maintaining a steady rhythm. Keep your movements fast and controlled, breathing steadily. For variety, perform double slams (both arms together) or jumping jacks with the ropes. Avoid hunching your shoulders or holding your breath.

name: Thrusters
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell in a front rack position (resting on your shoulders, elbows high, hands lightly gripping the bar) or holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Inhale as you lower into a squat, pushing your hips back and bending your knees until your thighs are parallel to the ground. Exhale as you explosively stand up, using the momentum to press the barbell or dumbbells overhead, fully extending your arms. Inhale as you lower the weight back to your shoulders and immediately descend into the next squat. Avoid letting your knees cave inward or your back round.

name: Wall Balls
instructions: Stand about two feet from a wall, holding a medicine ball at chest level, feet shoulder-width apart. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Inhale as you lower into a squat, pushing your hips back and bending your knees until your thighs are parallel to the ground. Exhale as you explosively stand up, using the momentum to throw the ball up toward a target on the wall (typically 9-10 feet high). Catch the ball as it comes down, immediately descending into the next squat. Keep your movements fluid and avoid letting your knees cave inward or your back round.

name: Hip Circles
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, hands on your hips. Engage your core and keep your upper body stable. Begin circling your hips in a clockwise motion, pushing them forward, to the right, back, and to the left, as if drawing a large circle with your hips. Perform for the desired duration, then switch to counterclockwise circles. Keep your movements smooth and controlled, focusing on mobilizing your hips. Avoid arching your back or moving your upper body excessively.

name: Thoracic Spine Rotation
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs crossed, or kneel if more comfortable. Place your right hand on your left knee and your left hand on the floor behind you for support. Engage your core and keep your spine tall. Exhale as you rotate your torso to the left, looking over your left shoulder, feeling a stretch in your upper back. Hold for a moment, then inhale as you return to the starting position. Repeat on the other side. Avoid forcing the rotation or rounding your back.

name: Kettlebell Goblet Squat
instructions: Stand with your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out, holding a kettlebell at chest level with both hands, elbows pointing down. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Inhale as you push your hips back and bend your knees, lowering into a squat until your thighs are at least parallel to the ground. Keep your knees tracking over your toes and your back straight. Exhale as you drive through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top. Avoid letting your knees cave inward or your chest collapse forward.

name: Kettlebell Snatch
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, a kettlebell on the ground between your feet. Bend at your hips and knees to grasp the kettlebell with your right hand, keeping your back flat and core engaged. Inhale as you pull the kettlebell off the ground, driving through your hips to swing it up in a smooth arc. As the kettlebell reaches chest height, quickly flip your wrist so the kettlebell rotates over your hand, catching it overhead with your arm fully extended. Exhale as you stand tall, then lower the kettlebell back to the starting position with control. Repeat on the other side. Avoid rounding your back or using your arm to yank the kettlebell.

name: Wall Sit
instructions: Stand with your back against a wall, feet shoulder-width apart and about two feet away from the wall. Engage your core and slide down the wall until your thighs are parallel to the ground, knees at a 90-degree angle, and directly over your ankles. Keep your back flat against the wall and your hands off your thighs (place them on your hips or let them hang by your sides). Hold this position, breathing steadily, without letting your knees cave inward or your back round. For a regression, don’t slide down as far.

name: Handstand Push-Ups
instructions: Start in a handstand position against a wall, with your hands shoulder-width apart, fingers pointing forward, and heels lightly touching the wall for support. Engage your core and keep your body in a straight line from hands to feet. Inhale as you bend your elbows, lowering your head toward the ground in a controlled motion, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your body. Exhale as you press back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms. Avoid letting your elbows flare out excessively or your back arch. For a regression, perform with your feet on a box in a pike position.

name: Pike Push-Ups
instructions: Start in a downward dog position with your hands shoulder-width apart, hips raised, and body forming an inverted V-shape, feet hip-width apart. Engage your core and keep your neck neutral. Inhale as you bend your elbows, lowering your head toward the ground in a controlled motion, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your body and focusing on your shoulders. Exhale as you press back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms. Avoid letting your hips sag or your elbows flare out excessively. This is a regression from handstand push-ups.

name: Single Leg Deadlift
instructions: Stand on your left leg, holding a dumbbell in your right hand, with your right leg slightly bent and lifted off the ground behind you. Keep your back flat and core engaged. Inhale as you hinge at your hips, lowering the dumbbell toward the ground while extending your right leg straight back, maintaining balance. Lower until your torso is parallel to the ground, then exhale as you return to the starting position, squeezing your glutes. Switch sides. Avoid rounding your back or letting your standing knee cave inward.

name: Superman Hold
instructions: Lie face down on a mat with your arms extended in front of you, legs straight, and neck in a neutral position. Engage your core and squeeze your glutes. Exhale as you simultaneously lift your arms, chest, and legs off the ground, keeping your gaze slightly forward to avoid straining your neck. Hold this position for a few seconds, feeling the contraction in your lower back and glutes, then inhale as you lower back down with control. Avoid overarching your back or shrugging your shoulders. For beginners, start with 10-15 seconds.

name: Good Morning Barbell
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back (on your traps, not your neck), hands gripping the bar just outside your shoulders. Keep a slight bend in your knees and engage your core. Inhale as you hinge at your hips, pushing your hips back and lowering your torso until it’s nearly parallel to the ground, feeling a stretch in your hamstrings. Keep your back flat and neck neutral. Exhale as you drive your hips forward to return to the starting position, squeezing your glutes at the top. Avoid rounding your back or letting your knees bend excessively.

name: Walking Lunges
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, hands on your hips or by your sides. Step forward with your right leg, lowering your body into a lunge until both knees form 90-degree angles, keeping your front knee over your ankle and your back knee just above the ground. Exhale as you push through your right heel to stand and bring your left leg forward into the next lunge. Continue alternating legs, keeping your torso upright and core engaged. Avoid letting your front knee extend past your toes or your back knee touch the ground.

name: Walking Lunges with Dumbbells
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides, palms facing your body. Step forward with your right leg, lowering your body into a lunge until both knees form 90-degree angles, keeping your front knee over your ankle and your back knee just above the ground. Exhale as you push through your right heel to stand and bring your left leg forward into the next lunge. Continue alternating legs, keeping your torso upright and core engaged. Avoid letting your front knee extend past your toes or your back knee touch the ground.

name: Wide-Grip Lat Pull-Down
instructions: Sit at a lat pull-down machine and adjust the seat so your thighs are secured under the pads. Grasp the bar with a wide overhand grip, hands wider than shoulder-width. Sit upright, engage your core, and lean back slightly (about 20 degrees). Exhale as you pull the bar down toward your upper chest, driving your elbows down and squeezing your lats. Pause briefly, then inhale as you return the bar to the starting position with control. Avoid pulling the bar behind your neck or using momentum to pull the weight.

name: Wide Push Up
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands placed wider than shoulder-width apart, fingers pointing forward, and body in a straight line from head to heels. Engage your core and keep your hips level. Inhale as you lower your chest toward the floor, bending your elbows outward, until your chest nearly touches the ground. Exhale as you push back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms. Focus on engaging your chest and shoulders. Avoid letting your hips sag or your shoulders shrug.

name: Kneeling Ab Wheel Rollout
instructions: Kneel on a mat with your feet crossed behind you, holding an ab wheel directly under your shoulders with both hands, arms extended. Engage your core by pulling your belly button toward your spine. Inhale as you slowly roll the wheel forward, extending your body while keeping your back straight and hips stable. Roll out as far as you can without letting your hips sag or your back arch, then exhale as you pull the wheel back to the starting position by contracting your abs. For beginners, roll out only a short distance to maintain control.

name: Reverse Fly
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand. Hinge at your hips to lean forward slightly, keeping your back flat and core engaged, letting the dumbbells hang below your shoulders, palms facing each other. Exhale as you raise your arms out to the sides, squeezing your rear delts and upper back, keeping a slight bend in your elbows. Inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with control. Avoid shrugging your shoulders or using momentum to lift the weights.

name: Wall Push Ups
instructions: Stand about arm’s length away from a wall, feet hip-width apart. Place your hands on the wall at shoulder height, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, fingers pointing up. Engage your core and keep your body in a straight line from head to heels. Inhale as you bend your elbows, lowering your chest toward the wall, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your torso. Exhale as you push back to the starting position, fully extending your arms. Avoid letting your hips sag or your shoulders shrug. This is a great beginner variation to build strength for floor push-ups.

name: Chair Squats
instructions: Stand in front of a sturdy chair with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Inhale as you push your hips back and bend your knees, lowering your body until you lightly touch the chair with your glutes, keeping your knees tracking over your toes. Exhale as you drive through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top. Avoid letting your knees cave inward or your back round. Use the chair for support if needed by holding the backrest.

name: Knee Plank
instructions: Start on your knees with your forearms on the ground, elbows directly under your shoulders, and body in a straight line from head to knees. Engage your core by pulling your belly button toward your spine, and keep your back flat. Hold this position, breathing steadily, without letting your hips sag or rise. Keep your neck neutral by looking at the floor just ahead of you. For beginners, start with 10-20 seconds and gradually increase the duration as you build strength.

name: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press
instructions: Sit on a chair or bench with your back straight, feet flat on the floor, holding a light dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height, palms facing forward, elbows bent at 90 degrees. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Exhale as you press the dumbbells overhead, bringing them close together at the top without locking your elbows. Inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your torso. Avoid shrugging your shoulders or arching your back. Use light weights to start.

name: Standing Hip Circles
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, hands on your hips. Engage your core and keep your upper body still. Slowly circle your hips in a clockwise direction, making large, controlled circles, as if drawing a hula hoop motion with your hips. Complete the desired number of reps, then switch to counterclockwise circles. Focus on smooth, controlled movements to warm up your hips and improve mobility. Avoid jerking or overextending your lower back.

name: Seated Machine Chest Press
instructions: Sit on a chest press machine with your back against the pad, feet flat on the floor, and hands grasping the handles at chest height, palms facing forward. Adjust the seat so the handles are at mid-chest level. Engage your core and keep your shoulders relaxed. Exhale as you press the handles forward until your arms are fully extended but not locked out. Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position with control, feeling a stretch in your chest. Avoid shrugging your shoulders or arching your back.

name: Jump Rope
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a jump rope with both hands, elbows close to your sides. Engage your core and keep your shoulders relaxed. Swing the rope over your head and jump over it with both feet, landing softly on the balls of your feet. Continue at a steady pace, keeping your jumps low to the ground and your breathing steady. For beginners, start with 20-30 seconds and rest as needed. Avoid jumping too high or tensing your shoulders.

name: High Knees
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, arms by your sides. Engage your core and begin jogging in place, lifting your knees as high as possible toward your chest with each step. Pump your arms in sync with your legs, keeping your movements quick and controlled. Land softly on the balls of your feet and maintain an upright posture. Perform at a moderate pace for beginners, focusing on form rather than speed. Avoid slouching or overstriding.

name: Mountain Climbers
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders, body in a straight line from head to heels, and core engaged. Quickly drive your right knee toward your chest, then return it to the starting position as you simultaneously drive your left knee toward your chest. Continue alternating legs at a steady pace, keeping your hips level and breathing rhythmically. For beginners, slow down the pace to maintain control and avoid letting your hips rise too high.

name: Bicycle Crunches
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent, feet off the floor, and hands lightly behind your ears. Engage your core by pulling your belly button toward your spine. Lift your shoulder blades off the ground and bring your right elbow toward your left knee while extending your right leg straight out, hovering above the ground. Switch sides by bringing your left elbow toward your right knee while extending your left leg. Continue alternating sides in a pedaling motion, keeping your movements controlled. Avoid pulling on your neck or using momentum.

name: Russian Twists
instructions: Sit on the floor with your knees bent, feet flat on the ground (or lifted for more challenge), and lean back slightly to engage your core, keeping your back straight. Hold a dumbbell or weight with both hands at chest level. Engage your core and twist your torso to the right, bringing the weight toward the floor beside your hip. Twist to the left, moving the weight to the other side. Continue alternating sides in a controlled motion, keeping your core tight and breathing steadily. Avoid rounding your back or using your arms to swing the weight.

name: Seated Dumbbell Row
instructions: Sit on a bench or chair with your feet flat on the floor, holding a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other. Lean forward slightly at your hips, keeping your back flat and core engaged, letting the dumbbells hang straight down. Exhale as you row the dumbbells up toward your waist, driving your elbows back and squeezing your shoulder blades together. Inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with control. Avoid rounding your back or shrugging your shoulders.

name: Cable Bicep Curl
instructions: Stand at a cable machine with a straight bar attachment set at the lowest position. Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart, and stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, elbows tucked into your sides. Engage your core and keep your shoulders relaxed. Exhale as you curl the bar toward your chest, focusing on squeezing your biceps and keeping your upper arms stationary. Inhale as you slowly lower the bar back to the starting position, maintaining tension in your biceps. Avoid swinging the weight or leaning back.

name: Dumbbell Lunges
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides, palms facing your body. Step forward with your right leg, lowering your body into a lunge until both knees form 90-degree angles, keeping your front knee over your ankle and your back knee just above the ground. Exhale as you push through your right heel to return to the starting position. Repeat on the left leg, alternating sides. Keep your torso upright and core engaged. Avoid letting your front knee extend past your toes.

name: Side Plank
instructions: Lie on your right side with your legs straight, propping yourself up on your right forearm, elbow directly under your shoulder. Stack your feet or place your left foot in front of your right for more stability. Engage your core and lift your hips off the ground, forming a straight line from head to heels. Hold this position, breathing steadily, without letting your hips sag. Switch to the left side after the desired duration. For beginners, start with 10-15 seconds per side and use your knees for support if needed.

name: Cat-Cow Stretch
instructions: Start on your hands and knees in a tabletop position, with your wrists under your shoulders and knees under your hips. Inhale as you drop your belly toward the floor, lifting your chest and tailbone toward the ceiling (cow pose), keeping your gaze forward. Exhale as you round your back, tucking your chin to your chest and pulling your belly button toward your spine (cat pose). Move slowly between these two positions, syncing your breath with the movement, to warm up your spine and improve mobility. Avoid forcing the stretch or overextending your neck.

name: Standing Calf Raises
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, hands on your hips or lightly resting on a wall for balance. Engage your core and keep your posture upright. Exhale as you rise onto the balls of your feet, squeezing your calves at the top of the movement. Pause briefly, then inhale as you lower your heels back to the ground with control. For added challenge, perform on a step with your heels hanging off the edge to increase the range of motion. Avoid bouncing or using momentum to lift.

name: Wall Sit
instructions: Stand with your back against a wall, feet about 2 feet away from the wall, hip-width apart. Slide down the wall by bending your knees until your thighs are parallel to the ground, forming a 90-degree angle at your knees. Keep your back flat against the wall, core engaged, and hands resting on your thighs or by your sides. Hold this position, breathing steadily, without letting your knees extend past your toes. For beginners, start with 15-20 seconds and gradually increase the duration.

name: Incline Push Ups
instructions: Place your hands on a sturdy elevated surface (like a bench or step), slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, fingers pointing forward. Step your feet back so your body forms a straight line from head to heels, core engaged. Inhale as you bend your elbows, lowering your chest toward the surface, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle to your torso. Exhale as you push back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms. Avoid letting your hips sag or your shoulders shrug. This is a great progression from wall push-ups.

name: Seated Dumbbell Bicep Curl
instructions: Sit on a chair or bench with your back straight, feet flat on the floor, holding a dumbbell in each hand, arms by your sides, palms facing forward. Keep your elbows tucked into your torso and your shoulders relaxed. Exhale as you curl the dumbbells toward your shoulders, focusing on squeezing your biceps and keeping your upper arms stationary. Pause at the top, then inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, maintaining control. Avoid swinging the weights or using your shoulders to lift.

name: Dumbbell Goblet Squat
instructions: Stand with your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out, holding a dumbbell at chest level with both hands, elbows pointing down. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Inhale as you push your hips back and bend your knees, lowering into a squat until your thighs are at least parallel to the ground. Keep your knees tracking over your toes and your back straight. Exhale as you drive through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top. Avoid letting your knees cave inward or your chest collapse forward.

name: Bird Dog
instructions: Start on your hands and knees in a tabletop position, with your wrists under your shoulders and knees under your hips. Engage your core to keep your spine neutral. Exhale as you extend your right arm forward and your left leg straight back, keeping both parallel to the ground, balancing on your left hand and right knee. Hold for a moment, then inhale as you return to the starting position. Switch sides, extending your left arm and right leg. Avoid arching your back or letting your hips rotate—keep your movements controlled.

name: Seated Leg Press
instructions: Sit on a leg press machine with your back and hips against the seat, feet shoulder-width apart on the platform, toes slightly turned out. Adjust the seat so your knees are at a 90-degree angle. Grasp the handles for stability and engage your core. Exhale as you press through your heels to extend your legs, stopping just before locking your knees. Inhale as you slowly lower the platform back down until your knees are at a 90-degree angle again. Keep your hips and back against the seat and avoid rounding your lower back.

name: Cable Woodchoppers
instructions: Set a cable machine to the highest position and attach a single handle. Stand sideways to the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding the handle with both hands. Engage your core and keep your arms extended. Exhale as you pull the handle diagonally across your body toward your opposite hip, rotating your torso and pivoting your back foot for stability. Inhale as you return to the starting position with control. Perform on both sides. Avoid using your arms to pull—focus on rotating through your core.

name: Dumbbell Step-Ups
instructions: Stand in front of a sturdy step or platform (knee height or lower for beginners), holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides. Step your right foot fully onto the platform, pressing through your heel to lift your body up, bringing your left foot to meet your right. Step back down with your left foot, then your right, keeping your torso upright and core engaged. Alternate legs or complete all reps on one side before switching. Avoid bouncing or using momentum to step up.

name: Side Lateral Raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a light dumbbell in each hand by your sides, palms facing in. Engage your core and keep your shoulders relaxed. Exhale as you raise your arms out to the sides to shoulder height, keeping a slight bend in your elbows to avoid strain. Pause briefly at the top, then inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to your sides with control. Avoid swinging the weights or shrugging your shoulders toward your ears. Use light weights for beginners.

name: Burpees
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, arms by your sides. Squat down and place your hands on the floor in front of you, then jump your feet back into a high plank position, keeping your core engaged. Perform a push-up (optional for beginners), then jump your feet back toward your hands. From the squat position, explode upward into a jump, reaching your arms overhead. Land softly and immediately go into the next rep. Keep your movements controlled and breathe rhythmically. For beginners, step back instead of jumping.

name: Jumping Jacks
instructions: Stand with your feet together, arms by your sides. Engage your core and jump your feet out to the sides while simultaneously raising your arms overhead, forming a star shape with your body. Jump back to the starting position, bringing your feet together and arms back to your sides. Continue at a steady pace, landing softly on the balls of your feet and keeping your breathing steady. For beginners, step one foot out at a time instead of jumping to reduce impact.

name: Leg Raises
instructions: Lie on your back with your legs straight, arms by your sides or under your glutes for support, and lower back pressed into the floor. Engage your core by pulling your belly button toward your spine. Exhale as you lift your legs straight up until they’re perpendicular to the floor, keeping them together. Inhale as you slowly lower your legs back down, stopping just above the ground without letting your lower back arch. For beginners, bend your knees slightly to reduce intensity.

name: Seated Calf Raise Machine
instructions: Sit on a seated calf raise machine with your thighs under the pads, balls of your feet on the platform, and heels hanging off the edge. Grasp the handles for stability and keep your back straight. Exhale as you press through the balls of your feet, raising your heels as high as possible and squeezing your calves at the top. Inhale as you lower your heels back down with control, feeling a stretch in your calves. Avoid bouncing or using momentum to lift the weight.

name: Dumbbell Front Squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height, palms facing in, elbows pointing down. Engage your core and keep your chest up. Inhale as you push your hips back and bend your knees, lowering into a squat until your thighs are at least parallel to the ground. Keep your knees tracking over your toes and your back straight. Exhale as you drive through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top. Avoid letting your knees cave inward or your chest collapse forward.

name: Reverse Lunges
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, hands on your hips or by your sides. Step your right leg back, lowering your body into a lunge until both knees form 90-degree angles, keeping your front knee over your ankle and your back knee just above the ground. Exhale as you push through your left heel to return to the starting position. Repeat on the left leg, alternating sides. Keep your torso upright and core engaged. Avoid letting your front knee extend past your toes or your back knee touch the ground.

name: Dumbbell Reverse Lunges
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides, palms facing your body. Step your right leg back, lowering your body into a lunge until both knees form 90-degree angles, keeping your front knee over your ankle and your back knee just above the ground. Exhale as you push through your left heel to return to the starting position. Repeat on the left leg, alternating sides. Keep your torso upright and core engaged. Avoid letting your front knee extend past your toes.

name: Cable Tricep Kickback
instructions: Set a cable machine to the lowest position and attach a single handle. Stand facing the machine, grabbing the handle with your right hand, palm facing in. Step back and hinge at your hips, leaning forward slightly, keeping your back flat and core engaged. Pin your right elbow to your side, upper arm parallel to the ground. Exhale as you extend your arm straight back, contracting your triceps. Inhale as you return to the starting position with control. Repeat on the other side. Avoid swinging the weight or moving your upper arm.

name: Dumbbell Tricep Kickback
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand. Hinge at your hips to lean forward slightly, keeping your back flat and core engaged. Bend your elbows to bring the dumbbells up to your sides, upper arms parallel to the ground. Exhale as you extend your arms straight back, squeezing your triceps at the end of the movement. Inhale as you return to the starting position with control, keeping your upper arms stationary. Avoid swinging the weights or rounding your back.

name: Standing Arm Swings
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, arms relaxed by your sides. Engage your core and keep your posture upright. Begin swinging your arms forward and backward in a controlled motion, gradually increasing the range of motion to warm up your shoulders. After 15-20 seconds, switch to crossing your arms in front of your chest and then opening them wide to the sides, forming a T-shape. Continue for another 15-20 seconds, keeping your movements smooth and breathing steadily. Avoid jerky motions or shrugging your shoulders.

name: Seated Machine Row
instructions: Sit on a seated row machine with your chest against the pad, feet flat on the floor, and hands grasping the handles with a neutral grip. Adjust the seat so your arms are fully extended when holding the handles. Engage your core and pull your shoulder blades back. Exhale as you pull the handles toward your torso, driving your elbows back and squeezing your shoulder blades together. Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position with control. Avoid rounding your back or shrugging your shoulders.

name: Flutter Kicks
instructions: Lie on your back with your legs straight, arms by your sides or under your glutes for support, and lower back pressed into the floor. Engage your core by pulling your belly button toward your spine. Lift your legs slightly off the ground (about 6 inches), keeping them straight. Begin fluttering your legs up and down in small, controlled motions, alternating one leg up while the other goes down. Keep your breathing steady and avoid letting your lower back arch off the floor. For beginners, keep your legs higher to reduce intensity.

name: Dead Bug
instructions: Lie on your back with your arms extended toward the ceiling, knees bent at 90 degrees, and shins parallel to the floor. Engage your core by pressing your lower back into the floor. Exhale as you extend your right arm overhead and your left leg straight toward the ground, keeping your lower back pressed down. Inhale as you return to the starting position, then switch sides, extending your left arm and right leg. Move slowly to maintain control and avoid arching your back.

name: Cable Face Pull
instructions: Set a cable machine with a rope attachment at upper chest height. Stand facing the machine, feet shoulder-width apart, and grab the rope with both hands, palms facing each other. Step back until your arms are fully extended, then lean back slightly (about 20 degrees) and engage your core. Pull the rope toward your face, flaring your elbows out to the sides and aiming the handles toward your forehead. Squeeze your rear delts and upper back at the end of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position. Keep your shoulders down and avoid using your traps excessively.

name: Dumbbell Pullover
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet planted on the ground, holding a dumbbell with both hands above your chest, gripping the handle, palms facing each other, elbows slightly bent. Engage your core and keep your back flat against the bench. Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbell back over your head in a controlled arc, keeping your arms stable and feeling a stretch in your chest and lats. Lower until the dumbbell is parallel to the floor, then exhale as you return to the starting position, squeezing your chest and lats. Avoid letting your lower back arch or your elbows flare out excessively.

name: Hip Thrust
instructions: Sit on the floor with your upper back against a bench, knees bent, and feet flat on the ground, hip-width apart. Engage your core and keep your chin tucked. Exhale as you drive through your heels, lifting your hips until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees, squeezing your glutes at the top. Inhale as you lower your hips back down with control, stopping just above the ground. Avoid overarching your lower back or letting your knees cave inward. For beginners, practice the movement without weight.

name: Seated Dumbbell Lateral Raise
instructions: Sit on a chair or bench with your back straight, feet flat on the floor, holding a light dumbbell in each hand by your sides, palms facing in. Engage your core and keep your shoulders relaxed. Exhale as you raise your arms out to the sides to shoulder height, keeping a slight bend in your elbows to avoid strain. Pause briefly at the top, then inhale as you lower the dumbbells back to your sides with control. Avoid swinging the weights or shrugging your shoulders toward your ears.

name: Cable Chest Fly
instructions: Set the cable machine pulleys to chest height. Stand in the center, grabbing a handle in each hand with an overhand grip. Step forward into a staggered stance (one foot slightly ahead), keeping a slight bend in your elbows. Engage your core and maintain a slight forward lean. Exhale as you squeeze your chest muscles and bring the handles together in front of your chest, arms forming a wide arc. Pause briefly when the handles meet, then inhale as you slowly return to the starting position, keeping the movement controlled. Avoid letting your shoulders shrug or your back arch excessively.

name: Box Jumps
instructions: Stand in front of a sturdy box or platform (start with a low height for beginners, around 12-18 inches). Feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, arms relaxed by your sides. Engage your core and swing your arms back as you bend your knees to prepare for the jump. Exhale as you explode upward, swinging your arms forward, and land softly on the box with both feet, knees slightly bent. Step back down one foot at a time and reset. Avoid landing with straight legs or jumping off the box—step down to reduce impact.
